task_completion.description: The wildlife_gallery directory and thumbs subdirectory are created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file manager or terminal window showing the creation or existence of '/home/ga/Documents/wildlife_gallery/' and its nested 'thumbs/' folder.

task_completion.description: The three image thumbnails are saved in the thumbs directory with their original filenames.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'thumbs' folder containing exactly 'monarch_butterfly.jpg', 'red_eyed_tree_frog.jpg', and 'galapagos_tortoise.jpg'.

task_completion.description: The thumbnails are resized so that neither width nor height exceeds 200 pixels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal commands (e.g., ImageMagick's convert/mogrify with -resize 200x200), an image editor GUI, or file manager properties confirming the images in the 'thumbs' directory were actually scaled down to a maximum of 200 pixels.

task_completion.description: The HTML gallery contains a visible heading with the exact text 'Wildlife Gallery'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an HTML editor showing an <h1> to <h6> tag containing 'Wildlife Gallery', or a web browser rendering the page with 'Wildlife Gallery' as a prominent heading.

task_completion.description: The HTML gallery displays the three thumbnails.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a web browser rendering 'index.html' and displaying the three wildlife images (note: two of them might look like synthetic plasma fractals). The HTML code should show tags pointing to the 'thumbs/' directory.

task_completion.description: Each thumbnail is wrapped in a hyperlink pointing to its corresponding high-resolution original image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for HTML source code showing tags with href attributes pointing to the original '/home/ga/Documents/wildlife_photos/' directory wrapping the tags, or a web browser showing the target URL in the status bar when hovering over a thumbnail.

integrity.description: The agent created actual resized thumbnail files rather than just using HTML width/height attributes to scale the original images.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the tags in the HTML point to the 'thumbs/' directory and that the agent used an image processing tool or script to generate these files.

integrity.description: The agent actively wrote or generated the HTML file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the agent using a text editor (like nano, vim, gedit, or VS Code) or a script to author the 'index.html' file.

integrity.description: The hyperlinks point to the original high-resolution images, not the thumbnails.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify in the HTML code or browser hover state that the href paths point to 'wildlife_photos' and the src paths point to 'thumbs'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating an HTML gallery at '/home/ga/Documents/wildlife_gallery/index.html' with a 'thumbs' subdirectory. The three images are 'monarch_butterfly.jpg', 'red_eyed_tree_frog.jpg', and 'galapagos_tortoise.jpg'. Note: The monarch butterfly and galapagos tortoise images might appear as synthetic 1920x1080 plasma fractals instead of actual animals due to a download fallback. Thumbnails must be actual resized files (max 200x200 pixels) in the 'thumbs' directory, not just HTML-scaled images. Each thumbnail must be hyperlinked to the original high-resolution image in '/home/ga/Documents/wildlife_photos/'.
